page: Vultr vcg-a16-96c-878g-256vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with an x86_64 architecture AMD processor providing 96 shared vCPUs. The system is equipped with 877.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 9.14 GB of RAM per core.

This server features a dense GPU configuration consisting of 16 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 256 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 3072 GB NVMe SSD, which supports high-speed data access. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the high density of 16 GPUs and substantial memory capacity, this hardware profile is designed for resource-intensive workloads. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for pure CPU-bound tasks, but the massive GPU VRAM and NVMe storage capacity make it highly efficient for parallel processing tasks. Appropriate workloads include large-scale virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) hosting, video transcoding, machine learning inference, and high-density graphics rendering.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-96c-878g-256vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server featuring an x86_64 AMD processor with 96 shared vCPUs and 877.0 GB of system memory. The server is equipped with 16 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, providing a total of 256 GB of VRAM, alongside 3072 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. This hardware configuration is designed for highly parallelized workloads that require significant graphics processing power and memory capacity. While the shared CPU allocation is a consideration for CPU-intensive tasks, the dense GPU array and substantial NVMe storage make this server suitable for high-density virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI), video streaming, and machine learning inference workloads.

tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring sixteen NVIDIA Ampere accelerators and substantial NVMe storage for parallel processing and virtual desktop infrastructure.

877.0 GB of system memory providing 9.14 GB per core
